input.headline: Bok coach Rassie: 'I would be lying if I said we don't want to tick the Ireland box'

raw.headline: Bok coach Rassie: 'I would be lying if I said we don't want to tick the Ireland box'

winner: Rassie, after hearing about Ireland's famous leprechauns, confessed, 'I would be lying if I said we don't want to find the elusive four-leaf clover box!'

candidates.text: If Ireland was a checkbox, I'd need a new pen after filling it out - that's how eager we Boks are!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative twist on the 'checkbox' metaphor, ties well to the headline, concise and surprising."}

candidates.text: Why did Rassie bring a checklist on the rugby field? To make sure they could tick the Ireland box!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat related to the headline and uses the 'tick the Ireland box' phrase creatively, but it lacks specificity and surprise. The reference to a checklist on the rugby field feels a bit forced and generic."}

candidates.text: Rassie: 'We wouldn't be here if we didn't want to check that particular box...the post office one.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke attempts to twist the original quote with a pun on 'box,' but the reference to the post office feels forced and lacks the sharp surprise needed for maximum humor."}

candidates.text: Rassie admitted, 'Ireland has been a thorn in our side for too long. So, if I'm being honest, we don't want to tick the Bok box anymore!'
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke plays on the mishearing of 'Bok box' instead of 'Ireland box,' which is a clever twist. However, it's somewhat predictable and lacks the sharp surprise or specificity that would elevate it further."}

candidates.text: Rassie, after hearing about Ireland's famous leprechauns, confessed, 'I would be lying if I said we don't want to find the elusive four-leaf clover box!'
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat creative with the four-leaf clover twist, but it's a bit forced and doesn't fully align with the original headline's context. It lacks specificity in connecting to rugby or the 'Ireland box' reference."}
